observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit immediately the supporting documents to comply with the requirements of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the timely implementation of funded/programmed projects out of its MDF to hasten the delivery of services as well as to enable the municipality to fulfill its mandate and make full use of its financial resources.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan as mandated by law in order to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters and disaster risks, including projected climate risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis to the Audit Team in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and Management agreed to delimit its budget allocations and disbursements for discretionary expenses to the two percent limitation prescribed in the Local Government Code.

We also recommended and the Sangguniang Bayan, in the review of the proposed budget, agreed to take into consideration the limitations imposed under RA No. 7160 on certain items of expenditures.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Refrain from granting cash advances to elective officials except for their own official travelling expenses  in consonance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Grant special cash advances only to duly designated and bonded officers and employees.

Require the accountable officers to immediately liquidate the cash advances granted as prescribed under COA Circular 97-002;
Instruct the Municipal accountant to monitor closely that liquidation of the cash advance granted to accountable officer be made within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to immediately collect the excess per diems claimed by the participants and henceforth, strictly enforce the rules and regulations mentioned in EO No. 298.

We recommended that Management:
9.3.1	Conduct a needs assessment to determine trainings and similar activities that will contribute to the improvement of their capability development; and
9.3.2	Ensure that no study tours, training, programs and other similar activities will be implemented without the approved CapDev Agenda of the municipality and the proper endorsement of the DILG.

We reiterated the following recommendations and Management agreed that:
10.7.1	The municipality conduct a needs assessment to determine trainings and seminar to be attended and the participants to each training or seminar shall be limited to those whose functions are relevant to the said training/seminar.
10.7.2	Management to delimit their attendance to trainings, seminars, workshops or similar activities funded out of public funds, especially those held in places outside of their official stations or territorial jurisdictions.  Personnel of the Municipality are urged to take full cognizance of their principal responsibility and accountability relative to the prudent use of their scarce financial resources.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from disbursing reimbursement of communication expenses to its officials and employees through cash advance in accordance to the provisions of Section 3 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
12.4.1	Submit immediately supporting expense receipts for claims of reimbursement for communication expenses for CY 2017 to prevent the issuance of Notice of Suspensions or Notice of Disallowances; and
12.4.2	Consider availing of mobile communication post-paid plans to have a documentary trail of communication costs incurred by each official entitled to reimbursement of communication expense.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasury Office release the shares of the barangays from RPT collections within five days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 271 (d) of the LCG to afford the barangays timely implementation of their programs and projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that deductions from employees’ salaries should be effected only up to such extent that the net take home pay shall not be reduced below the required minimum taking into account the preferences stated under the GAA.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant keenly observe the deadline set for the submission of trial balances and other financial reports as required under Section 41(2) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended Management to immediately submit the financial reports with supporting documents covering the period February to August 2017. Henceforth, ensure that financial reports are submitted to the Audit Team within the period provided in COA Circular No. 2009-006.
We recommended that the Municipality amend its Revenue Code and reduce its tax rates in accordance with the maximum rates prescribed under Section 143 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan of the Municipality passed Municipal Ordinance No. 17-05 last June 13, 2017, amending the rates imposed in the 2014 Revenue Code of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Only Section 3 of the ordinance amending the tax on Retailers complied with RA No. 7160. Other amended rates are still more than the maximum rates prescribed by RA No. 7160.
